spec.reasoning: Risk flags indicate the task references absolute rows, deletes/inserts rows, depends on blank row positions, and has data values matching headers. Therefore: (1) We skip blank_row_insert since the task logic depends on blank row positions and deletes rows — inserting blank rows would change the semantics. (2) We skip title_row_insert per the risk flag for row deletion tasks. (3) We rename columns (Account→Acct, TransType→TxnType, Reference→Ref, Other→Misc) at the same descriptiveness level (abbreviation→abbreviation). We do NOT rename 'Line No' because the instruction explicitly references 'Line No' as a semantic keyword used in the macro logic (rows containing the term 'Line No'). Renaming it would require changing the instruction's core logic description. (4) We rename Sheet1→DataSheet (full word→full word). (5) We add a distractor column 'Notes' after the last column to test hardcoded column counts. (6) All data_value_renames cover cells where header names appear as data values, using the new sheet name 'DataSheet'. (7) instruction_paraphrase is false to avoid meaning drift — the instruction still references 'Line No' in column A, sheet 'BR1', rows 2 & 9, and range A1:E16, all of which remain correct since we only renamed non-Line-No columns and added a distractor column after E.
